item.title: Big Brother Is ReTruthing You
item.description: Donald Trump is a victim of propaganda as much as he is a manipulator of it.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: How AI Killed a 133-Year-Old Princeton Tradition
item.description: The school’s famous Honor Code was no match for chatbot-enabled cheating.
item.dc:creator: Rose Horowitch

item.title: Spencer Pratt and the Temptations of Populism
item.description: The long-shot candidate for L.A. mayor has run an effective campaign. Can he tap into populist energy without alienating Angelenos?
item.dc:creator: Conor Friedersdorf

item.title: The Unhappy Hosts of the World Cup
item.description: Cities and states are covering a lot of the costs of this summer’s matches, and have few options for bringing in much revenue.
item.dc:creator: Henry Grabar

item.title: What Did Jamaica Do to Deserve This?
item.description: Kari Lake, Trump’s pick for ambassador, has a disastrous record.
item.dc:creator: Anne Applebaum

item.title: Putin’s War Comes Home to Moscow
item.description: He can no longer hide the consequences from the Russian public.
item.dc:creator: Anne Applebaum

item.title: Send the Frigates
item.description: Despite their anger at Donald Trump, European nations have an interest in defending the freedom of navigation.
item.dc:creator: Eliot A. Cohen

item.title: I Remember America Before the Measles Vaccine
item.description: And I wish Robert F. Kennedy Jr. did too.
item.dc:creator: Fran Moreland Johns

item.title: People Who Don’t Like People Are Making All of Our Decisions
item.dc:creator: Xochitl Gonzalez
